name: Fair Value Gap
description: Displacement-and-retest model that waits for a fresh three-candle fair value gap, enters on the first respectful revisit of the gap, and exits into the next impulse or clear invalidation through the zone.

context: Bias with the direction of the displacement leg and nearby structure.
setup: Find a three-candle imbalance where candle three does not overlap candle one.
Wait for the first revisit into the gap after the displacement leg.
trigger: Long when the 30 EMA is above the 200 EMA, a recent bullish fair value gap exists, and price retests the gap while holding above its midpoint.
Short when the 30 EMA is below the 200 EMA, a recent bearish fair value gap exists, and price retests the gap while holding below its midpoint.
confirmation: Prefer the retest candle to reject the gap and close back in the displacement direction.
Use a clear entry arrow at the retest candle rather than chasing the initial impulse.
invalidation: Long invalidates if price accepts below the bullish gap low.
Short invalidates if price accepts above the bearish gap high.
noTrade: Skip weak gaps with no displacement or gaps that already filled cleanly before the first retest.

stopLoss: Place the stop beyond the far side of the gap.
takeProfit: Scale at the next opposing liquidity pool or use 2R as the default first target.
earlyExit: Exit early if price accepts through the gap instead of rejecting it.

checks.label: The 30 EMA is above the 200 EMA
A recent bullish fair value gap was retested and respected
The window range is wide enough to avoid compression
The 30 EMA is below the 200 EMA
A recent bearish fair value gap was retested and respected
The window range is wide enough to avoid compression
Price has already printed the upside impulse that completes the gap trade
Price has already printed the downside impulse that completes the gap trade
